raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "AB 689 will allow for survivors of domestic violence to seek help via text and other computer-based technologies.",
    "The Covid-19 Pandemic has led to a disturbing rise in domestic violence.",
    "In Sacramento, the Prosecutors Office has reported a 39% increase in police activity related to domestic violence over the last year.",
    "Currently, domestic violence centers are hampered by a limited definition for phone-based hotlines in California law.",
    "By modernizing domestic violence shelter requirements to include the option of other technological platforms, AB 689 allows domestic violence centers to be better equipped to help more survivors.",
    "Expanding the crisis line service to include texting or live chat gives survivors more options for accessing lifesaving services and support.",
    "Laura's House has implemented a secure chat line platform which has proven to be effective for communication with the youth and young adult population.",
    "Chat and text-based hotlines allow survivors to connect with advocates when they cannot safely call a hotline.",
    "Expanding access points to include chat and text-based options is key to breaking down barriers to survivor safety."
  ]
}
```
